text:
Well-Child Care | Sutter County, CA
Show/Hide
ROAD CLOSURES
COMMUNITY ACADEMY
Preventive well-child care is more than just routine check-ups. It's a way to keep your child healthy and strong. It involves going to the doctor regularly to make sure your child is growing well, staying healthy physically, emotionally, and mentally, and reaching all the important milestones as they grow up.
The American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP) recommends infants, children, and adolescents receive routine medical exams, also called well child exams or preventive healthcare.
It's important to choose a primary doctor for your child. This doctor will take care of your child's health needs and give them support as they grow.
Recommended ages for well-child visits:
2 weeks
2 months
4 months
6 months
9 months
12 months
15 months
18 months
2 years old
2.5 years old
3 years old
After the age of 3, yearly well-child visits are advised.
To learn more:
https://downloads.aap.org/AAP/PDF/periodicity_schedule.pdf
Benefits of Well-Child Care
Early Detection of Developmental Issues:
Regular well-child visits allow healthcare providers to monitor a child's growth and development, helping to identify any potential issues early on. Learn more:
https://www.cdc.gov/ncbddd/actearly/milestones/index.html
Immunizations Against Preventable Diseases:
Well-child care includes a schedule for vaccinations, protecting children from serious and preventable diseases. Learn more:
https://www.cdc.gov/vaccines/schedules/hcp/imz/child-adolescent.html
Promotion of Healthy Habits:
Well-child care emphasizes the importance of healthy lifestyle habits, including proper nutrition, regular physical activity, and sufficient sleep. Learn more:
https://www.cdc.gov/chronicdisease/resources/publications/factsheets/children-health.hm
Mental and Emotional Well-Being:
Identifying and addressing behavioral or emotional concerns early can contribute to positive mental health outcomes. Learn more:
https://www.cdc.gov/mentalhealth/children/index.htm
Oral Health
Regular dental check-ups for children are important to help prevent dental problems and keep teeth and gums healthy.
The AAP recommends having an established dental home for children by 12 months of age.
When your baby is 6 months, your pediatrician will start to do oral health checkups and apply fluoride varnish every 6 months until age 5.
Children should have dental exams and dental cleanings every 6 months.
Learn more:
https://www.cdc.gov/oralhealth/basics/childrens-oral-health/index.html
Services Available in Sutter County
If you need help finding a doctor or dentist, contact Sutter County Public Health:
Call (530) 822-7215
